summary: High-risk regulatory changes impacting food seasoning/snack compliance include: GCC/GSO introduced new mandatory labelling requirements for prepackaged foods (covering allergens, nutrition, ingredients), Halal food criteria, and mandatory shelf life declarations. Japan implemented stricter rules on additive use, production methods, and ingredient conformity to MHLW criteria, alongside new prohibitions on contaminated, harmful, or pathogen-laden foods, and limits for agricultural chemical residues. Singapore now mandates compliance with SFA regulatory limits for additives (requiring safety assessment) and contaminants (prohibiting import/sale if limits exceeded, including specific veterinary drug residues), with ingredient and additive declarations mandatory for prepacked foods. Taiwan requires mandatory allergen declarations for 11 specific allergens (including sulphite above 10 mg/kg) in prepackaged foods. Thailand has new requirements for allergen declarations, compliance with contaminant limits (including toxic substances and veterinary drug residues), general food and nutrition labeling, and pathogenic microorganism standards. The United States added sesame as the 9th major food allergen, set new action levels for total aflatoxins (20 ppb), and introduced stricter labeling for 'gluten-free' claims, cochineal/carmine, FD&C Yellow No. 5, sulfites (≥10 ppm), and all major allergens, while also emphasizing prohibited/delisted additives.

changes.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
changes.newLimit: Verification required from GSO 9:2022
changes.notes: This technical regulation covers mandatory labelling requirements, which typically include allergen declarations, nutritional information (including sodium), ingredients list, net content, and manufacturer details.

changes.newLimit: Verification required from GSO 2055-1:2015 for specific criteria
changes.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, impacting ingredients, processing, and labelling for Halal claims.

changes.parameter: Mandatory shelf life declaration for food products
changes.newLimit: Verification required from GSO 150-1:2013 for specific products/categories
changes.notes: This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products.

changes.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food/additives
changes.newLimit: Must conform to MHLW criteria
changes.notes: Food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to MHLW criteria.

changes.parameter: Contamination or foreign substances in food/additives
changes.newLimit: Prohibited for sale
changes.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, if likely to cause harm due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

changes.newLimit: Must conform to MHLW standards
changes.notes: Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW criteria must not be sold or imported. Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

changes.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, pharmaceuticals for animals
changes.newLimit: Must not exceed MHLW-specified quantities
changes.notes: Food containing residues exceeding MHLW-specified quantities (deemed unlikely to cause harm) must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved for sale, or sold. This does not apply if standards concerning ingredients of food under paragraph (1) have been established for the residual limit.

changes.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in food/additives
changes.newLimit: Prohibited for sale
changes.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless MHLW specifies they are unlikely to cause harm.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited for sale
changes.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, if contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and likely to cause harm.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited for sale
changes.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.

changes.newLimit: Mandatory compliance
Must be assessed for safety by SFA
changes.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

changes.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
changes.newLimit: Mandatory compliance
Must be within SFA's regulatory limits
Prohibition on import or sale
Prohibited
changes.notes: As defined under Regulation 29(3) of the Food Regulations, 'Veterinary drugs' include antibiotic and oestrogen.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on food labels
changes.notes: Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

changes.notes: Required for prepackaged foods containing this allergen.

changes.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues

Must comply with food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements
Must comply with standards for pathogenic microorganisms
Delisted (not permitted for use)
changes.notes: Refer to 21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30 for details.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited from use in food (with exceptions as noted in regulations)
changes.notes: Refer to 21 CFR Part 189 for details on prohibited substances.

changes.newLimit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
changes.notes: Sesame was added as the 9th major food allergen effective January 1, 2023, by the FASTER Act.
This is an Action Level. Specific action levels also exist for Brazil nuts, peanuts and peanut products, and pistachio nuts.

changes.newLimit: Must meet defined standard for gluten content. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
changes.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling and later established compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.

changes.newLimit: Establishments must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling
changes.notes: Part of the 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117).

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on the label
changes.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared in parentheses after ingredient name OR in a 'contains' statement immediately after/next to ingredient list. Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
changes.notes: This requirement is mandated by FALCPA and the FASTER Act.

changes.newLimit: Must be identified on labels
changes.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on food labels when concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites
changes.notes: Required due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals (e.g., asthma).

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
changes.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Specific provisions for food additives are available in the searchable GSFA Online Database, which is regularly updated. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and decisions by the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF)
changes.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) and Guideline Levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed to protect human health. These levels are recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted and are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and through the work of the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF).

changes.newLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
changes.notes: Codex has established Maximum Levels (MLs) for specific contaminants including mycotoxins, metals such as arsenic, lead, and mercury, and radionuclides. These MLs are designed to ensure food does not contain contaminants at levels which could threaten human health and are detailed in relevant Codex texts.
Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

changes.newLimit: Verification required (MHLW approval)
changes.notes: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives), preparations, and food containing additives must not be sold, produced, imported, processed, used, stored, or displayed for sale, except when MHLW specifies them as unlikely to cause harm.

changes.newLimit: Verification required
changes.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring 'certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site' must not be imported for sale unless accompanied by certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.

changes.newLimit: Verification required
changes.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring 'measures to control particularly important processes' must not be imported for sale unless produced/processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities that actually take those measures.

changes.parameter: General business operator responsibility for safety
changes.newLimit: N/A
changes.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure the safety of food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging, obtain necessary knowledge/skills, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, and take other necessary measures.

changes.newLimit: N/A
changes.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to take appropriate and prompt measures, such as providing records to authorities and disposing of hazardous food, to prevent food sanitation hazards.

changes.newLimit: N/A
changes.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to record and retain necessary information, such as the name of the person who sold food/raw materials, to prevent food sanitation hazards.

changes.newLimit: Not required to be tested
changes.notes: This is an explicit statement about what is not required.

changes.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products (e.g., cuttlefish, neritic squid, octopus, squid)
changes.newLimit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: Recommended for prepackaged foods containing this allergen.

changes.parameter: Kiwis and kiwi products thereof (e.g., Kiwi jam, dried kiwi)
changes.newLimit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: Recommended for prepackaged foods containing this allergen.

changes.parameter: Seeds and seed products (e.g., Sunflower seed, melon seed)
changes.newLimit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive (excluding highly refined or purified sunflower seed oil)
changes.notes: Recommended for prepackaged foods containing this allergen. Highly refined or purified sunflower seed oil is not included.

changes.parameter: Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof (e.g., escargot, mussel, clam, oyster, scallop, mytilus, meretrix lusoria, abalone)
changes.newLimit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: Recommended for prepackaged foods containing this allergen.

changes.newLimit: Suggested statement: 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' or synonymous terms.
changes.notes: Suggested if there is a risk of cross-contamination from allergenic ingredients/additives.

changes.newLimit: Not required by law; should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices; must be truthful and not misleading
changes.notes: Such statements are used to address unavoidable 'cross-contact' only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes and taken every precaution.
